prompt: I'm building a Nostr social client in TypeScript. I want users to be able to log in by pasting a bunker:// URI from their remote signer. Write the complete connection flow using @nostr/tools — handle the initial connection, get the user's pubkey, handle auth challenges, and sign a kind:1 note. Also show how to persist the session so users stay logged in across page reloads.
assertions: Output imports BunkerSigner and parseBunkerInput from @nostr/tools/nip46 (or nostr-tools/nip46)
Output calls parseBunkerInput to parse the bunker:// URI
Output uses BunkerSigner.fromBunker() factory method (not constructor directly)
Output calls await bunker.connect() for the initial connection
Output calls await bunker.getPublicKey() after connecting and uses the returned value as the user identity
Output includes an onauth callback in BunkerSignerParams that handles auth challenge URLs
Output demonstrates session persistence by storing BunkerPointer and client secret key, and shows reconnection without calling connect() again
Output correctly distinguishes between remote-signer-pubkey (transport) and user-pubkey (identity)
